plan: In ForeignObject.deconstruct (django/db/models/fields/related.py, ~line 584), the string form of 'to' is built with self.remote_field.model.lower(), which lowercases the app label as well as the model name, so 'DJ_RegLogin.Category' becomes 'dj_reglogin.category' and the lazy reference can never resolve because app labels are case-sensitive. Fix by preserving the app label's case: if the string contains a dot, split into app_label and model_name and emit '%s.%s' % (app_label, model_name.lower()); only lowercase the whole string when there is no dot (bare model name). The class-object branch already uses _meta.label_lower, which keeps the app label's original case, so it needs no change.
